channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Senate GOP pushes Trump budget framework through after marathon vote series
item.description: An amended version of the House-passed Trump budget was voted on at the end of a marathon vote series in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Things to know about the US Mine Safety and Health Administration and the coal industry
item.description: The federal government wants the number of offices that oversee U.S. mine safety laws to align more with a shrinking coal industry.
item.dc:creator: John Raby And Leah Willingham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US consumers rush to buy big-ticket items before Trump's tariffs kick in
item.description: John Gutierrez had been thinking about buying a new laptop for the past year. The Austin, Texas, resident needed a computer with faster processing and increased storage for his photography work and had his sights set on a product from a Taiwanese brand.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH and MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Voices from coal country say closures of MSHA offices will endanger mine safety
item.description: Stanley “Goose” Stewart, who survived the Upper Big Branch coal mine disaster that killed 29 workers 15 years ago Saturday, calls a recommendation by the Trump administration to close three dozen offices of the Mine Safety and Health Administration across the country “idiotic.”.
item.dc:creator: John Raby And Leah Willingham, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Attorney General Ken Paxton’s former aides win $6.6 million in whistleblower case
item.description: Paxton vowed to appeal the “bogus ruling” that found he improperly fired the four plaintiffs after they reported him to the FBI on allegations of corruption.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels And Jasper Scherer, KPRC 2

item.title: IRS plans to cut up to 25% of staff, starting with closing its civil rights office, AP sources say
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The IRS plans to cut as many as 20,000 staffers — up to 25% of the workforce — as part of layoffs that began Friday, two people familiar with the situation told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress has the power to halt Trump's tariffs. But Republicans aren't ready to use it
item.description: Republicans in Congress are watching with unease as stock markets tumble in the aftermath of President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China hit brakes on TikTok deal after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump abruptly fires the 4-star general who headed the National Security Agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has abruptly fired the director of the National Security Agency, according to U.S. officials and members of Congress, but the White House and the Pentagon have provided no reasons for the move.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate over parental consent for minors' healthcare
item.description: A parental rights bill moving through the state legislature is sparking heated debate over access to reproductive healthcare for minors and whether or not parents should have a say.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese-owned GNC stores operating on US military bases spark national security concerns
item.description: Rep. Pat Harrigan warns GNC’s Chinese ownership poses a national security threat as the company operates 85 stores on U.S. military bases without disclosing ownership.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge orders Trump administration to return man Maryland mistakenly deported to El Salvador prison
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland ruled on Friday that a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador must be returned to the U.S. by Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump DOJ, Education Dept form task force to protect female athletes from 'gender ideology' in schools, sports
item.description: The Trump administration's Departments of Justice and Education have launched a joint special investigative unit for Title IX cases that will focus largely on the "pernicious effects of gender ideology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrat Rep Jared Golden says his party's moving in 'wrong direction' on trade
item.description: Rep. Jared Golden, D-Maine, is warning his own party not to have "knee-jerk" reactions to President Donald Trump's tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota rabbis say Trump administration’s tactics against antisemitism aren’t making Jewish people safer
item.description: The Minnesota Rabbinical Association released a letter on Tuesday calling the Trump administration’s immediate solution to curbing antisemitism “short-sighted.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing the “witch hunt” against her is yet another instance of critics in Europe using “lawfare” to quash free speech and censor political opposition.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The job market remains incredibly healthy -- but the tariff storm could upend things
item.description: Employers added 228,000 jobs in March, showcasing a solid labor market. But uncertainty over tariffs and tepid consumer spending may weigh on job growth in the months to come.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: PHOTOS: Biden’s ‘ambitious climate goals’ go down in literal flames by popular American beach
item.description: A major part of a first-of-its-kind green energy project, touted by Biden administration, is now lying in ruins and polluting some of America’s beautiful ocean and seashore in Massachusetts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan couple Paul, Christy Akeo freed from Mexico prison
item.description: Paul and Christy Akeo had been arrested in Cancún on March 4 after a high-end resort company accused them of fraud.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Senate advances blueprint for Trump tax cuts, other top priorities
item.description: The Senate on Thursday advanced its budget resolution, teeing up a weekend vote on a blueprint for President Trump's tax agenda and other top priorities. Senators voted 52-48 to kick off consideration of the measure. A simple majority was needed.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Social media platforms could be in for a popularity tax in Minnesota
item.description: A DFL bill would tax social media companies that access Minnesotans data. The lawmaker behind it said it could help bridge the state’s rocky budget outlook without adding costs for Minnesotans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: National Security Council staffers fired in wake of Trump meeting with Laura Loomer
item.description: Multiple staff members on the White House National Security Council were fired Thursday following a meeting President Trump had with far-right activist Laura Loomer where she raised questions about their loyalty.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Other DFL statewide officeholders break with Walz on return-to-office requirement
item.description: The attorney general, secretary of state and state auditor said they would keep existing telework rules in place because they are independent offices.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Fired or frustrated federal workers may have eager employers waiting for them in Minnesota
item.description: For federal workers, the past few months have been filled with uncertainty as the Trump administration pushes ahead with efforts to reduce the size of government agencies. But whether they are forced or choose to leave, those workers may find employers eager to hire them.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Proposed cut in local aid to prevent aquatic invasive species sparks fears of more infested lakes
item.description: For the past decade, Minnesota has distributed about $10 million a year to counties for aquatic invasive species prevention. Gov. Tim Walz’s proposed budget reduced that amount by 50 percent. Some lake advocates worry cutting the funding could potentially lead to more infested waters.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine wins special election for Florida's 6th Congressional District
item.description: Republican state Sen. Randy Fine won a special election Tuesday to represent Florida's 6th Congressional District, defeating Democratic challenger Josh Weil and squashing efforts by national Democrats who spent millions of dollars on the race.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Top scientists warn that Trump policies are causing a ‘climate of fear’ in research
item.description: Some 2,000 scientists, including dozens of Nobel Prize winners, have signed an open letter warning that the U.S. lead in science is being “decimated” by the Trump administration’s cuts to research.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman, MPR News

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
